summary: web3-secrets-detector ships wallet/credential scanner with hardcoded C2
details: Package contains scanner.js and wallet-scanner.js modules that perform host enumeration (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), os.* metadata) and filesystem scanning, then POST results to a hardcoded GitHub Pages endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. wallet-scanner.js explicitly walks the filesystem checking for wallet-related paths (fs.existsSync loops) and exfiltrates findings via https.request POST. build-verifier.js additionally invokes execSync('docker push...') and outbound https.request calls. The combination of hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 endpoint (ddjidd564.github.io is a personal GitHub Pages site, not a publisher domain), wallet-targeted filesystem enumeration, host fingerprinting via execSync/os.hostname, and curl/ping shell commands across multiple modules is a credential and wallet-secret stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
